 {"id":"bd-144k","title":"Fix help/keybinding component to render hints once","description":"## Problem
Keyboard navigation hints ("↑↓ navigate", "1-9 select", "z zoom", etc.) appear multiple times in the dashboard footer area due to content overflow.

## Screenshot Evidence
Multiple lines showing: "ti navigate 1-9 select z zoom r refresh ? help q quit"

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2084 - renderHelpBar() called
- internal/tui/components/help.go:63-110 - RenderHelpBar implementation

## Analysis
The RenderHelpBar function itself is correctly implemented:
- Uses lipgloss.Width() for width calculations (line 93-101)
- Returns a fresh string each call
- No state accumulation

The duplication occurs because:
1. Help bar is appended to main content without height management
2. When content overflows terminal, old frames with help bars remain visible
3. Same root cause as status bar duplication (bd-2usu)

## Fix Required
This is fixed by implementing proper footer region management (see bd-2usu). The help bar should be:
1. Part of the fixed footer region
2. Rendered after the content area is height-constrained
3. Not included in any scrollable viewport

## Additional Improvements
1. Ensure help hints are context-aware (show relevant hints for focused panel)
2. Add width-gated progressive disclosure (fewer hints on narrow terminals)
3. Consider sticky help bar that always shows essential hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help bar appears exactly once at the bottom
- [ ] Help hints update based on focused panel
- [ ] Narrow terminals show abbreviated hints
- [ ] No duplication on resize or scroll

## Unit Tests
1. TestHelpBar_RendersOnce - "navigate" appears exactly once in View()
2. TestHelpBar_ContextualHints - hints change based on focusedPanel
3. TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation - narrow width removes rightmost hints
4. TestHelpBar_NoAccumulation - multiple View() calls produce same output

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log number of hints rendered
- DEBUG: Log width truncation decisions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:39.445792941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.382075262Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.381982357Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14h3d","title":"Task: Implement FindingsVelocity metric","description":"# Task: FindingsVelocity Implementation

Track insights per token spent for efficiency analysis.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/velocity.go
type VelocityTracker struct {
    Entries []VelocityEntry
}

type VelocityEntry struct {
    ModeID        string
    ModeName      string
    TokensSpent   int
    FindingsCount int
    UniqueFindings int
    Velocity      float64 // findings per 1k tokens
}

type VelocityReport struct {
    Overall        float64 // total unique findings / total tokens * 1000
    PerMode        []VelocityEntry
    HighPerformers []string // modes above average
    LowPerformers  []string // modes below threshold
    Suggestions    []string
}

func NewVelocityTracker() *VelocityTracker
func (v *VelocityTracker) RecordOutput(modeID string, output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (v *VelocityTracker) CalculateVelocity() *VelocityReport
func (v *VelocityTracker) Render() string
```

## Velocity Calculation
```
velocity = (unique_findings / tokens_spent) * 1000

Unique findings are de-duplicated by:
- normalized finding text (lowercase + trim)
- evidence_pointer when present
```

## Inputs
- Token counts from BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator (per-mode)
- Outputs from normalized ModeOutput

## Edge Cases
- tokens_spent == 0 → skip or mark velocity as 0 with warning
- empty findings → velocity 0

## Visualization
```
Findings Velocity:
Overall: 2.3 findings / 1k tokens

Per Mode:
F1 Argument Mapping:    3.1 findings/1k (HIGH)
E2 Root Cause:          2.8 findings/1k
K1 Systems Thinking:    1.2 findings/1k (LOW)
L3 Meta-cognitive:      2.5 findings/1k
H2 Strategic:           1.9 findings/1k

Suggestion: K1 underperforming, consider early stop
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log tokens + findings per mode
- Log threshold crossings (below min velocity)

## Tests
- Unit: unique finding de-duplication
- Unit: zero-token handling
- Unit: per-mode and overall velocity math

 {"id":"bd-150ij","title":"AgentLauncher: Handle window:pane addressing (session:1.N format)","description":"## Purpose
Set working directory to the assigned project path before launching each agent, ensuring agents operate in the correct context.

## Implementation Details

```go
// PaneLauncher handles agent launching with directory setup
type PaneLauncher struct {
    tmux          *tmux.Client
    cmdBuilder    *LaunchCommandBuilder
    orchestrator  *session.SessionOrchestrator
    logger        *slog.Logger
}

// LaunchAgentInPane sets up and launches an agent in a specific pane
func (pl *PaneLauncher) LaunchAgentInPane(ctx context.Context, sessionName string, paneSpec PaneSpec) error {
    // Validate project path exists
    if _, err := os.Stat(paneSpec.Project); err != nil {
        pl.logger.Error("project path does not exist",
            "project", paneSpec.Project,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("project path %s: %w", paneSpec.Project, err)
    }
    
    pl.logger.Info("preparing pane for agent launch",
        "session", sessionName,
        "pane_index", paneSpec.Index,
        "project", paneSpec.Project,
        "agent_type", paneSpec.AgentType)
    
    // Get pane ID from orchestrator
    paneID, err := pl.orchestrator.GetPaneID(sessionName, paneSpec.Index)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("get pane ID: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Step 1: Change to project directory
    cdCmd := fmt.Sprintf("cd %q", paneSpec.Project)
    if err := pl.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, cdCmd, true); err != nil {
        pl.logger.Error("failed to cd to project",
            "pane", paneID,
            "project", paneSpec.Project,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("cd to project: %w", err)
    }
    
    pl.logger.Debug("changed directory",
        "pane", paneID,
        "project", paneSpec.Project)
    
    // Brief pause to ensure cd completes
    time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
    
    // Step 2: Build and send launch command
    launchCmd := pl.cmdBuilder.BuildLaunchCommand(paneSpec, paneSpec.Project)
    shellCmd := launchCmd.ToShellCommand()
    
    if err := pl.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, shellCmd, true); err != nil {
        pl.logger.Error("failed to launch agent",
            "pane", paneID,
            "command", shellCmd,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("launch agent: %w", err)
    }
    
    pl.logger.Info("agent launched successfully",
        "session", sessionName,
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", paneSpec.AgentType,
        "project", filepath.Base(paneSpec.Project),
        "command", shellCmd)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Integration with Existing Code
- Use internal/session.SessionOrchestrator for pane management
- Use internal/tmux.Client for SendKeys

## Logging Requirements
- Log project path validation
- Log directory change step
- Log launch command execution
- Include pane ID and session name in all logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validate project path exists before launch
- [ ] Change directory before agent launch
- [ ] Small delay between cd and launch for reliability
- [ ] Quote paths to handle spaces
- [ ] Comprehensive error logging
- [ ] Integration with SessionOrchestrator
- [ ] Unit tests with mock tmux client","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:25.679132890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:38:39.245327144Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:38:39.245167633Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-150ij","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-150ij","depends_on_id":"bd-3q59o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-151mi","title":"Task: Add robot wrapper for UBS scans","description":"## Overview
Expose `ntm --robot-ubs` (or `ntm ubs --json`) that runs UBS and returns normalized JSON.

## Requirements
- Pass through UBS exit code
- Include findings array with file/line/col/message

## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is stable and machine-parseable
- Missing UBS yields actionable error","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-15amw","title":"Task: REST pipeline endpoints","description":"## Scope\nExpose pipeline/workflow engine via REST.\n\n## Endpoints\n- /pipelines, /pipelines/run, /pipelines/{id}, /pipelines/{id}/cancel, /pipelines/{id}/resume, /pipelines/cleanup, /pipelines/exec, /pipelines/templates, /pipelines/validate.\n\n## Logging\n- Log pipeline IDs, step transitions, and failures.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: validation + error codes.\n- Integration: run pipeline and verify step events.\n- WebSocket: pipeline.started/step_completed/step_failed/complete events.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Pipeline runs are controllable via REST.\n- REST output aligns with CLI/robot pipeline output.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:51:23.320034452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:01:16.612446880Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:01:16.612426732Z","close_reason":"REST pipeline endpoints already implemented in internal/serve/pipelines.go with all required endpoints: list, run, get, cancel, resume, cleanup, exec, templates, validate","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15amw","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
